description: Create Python export script that extracts ONLY the GTCRN encoder/decoder neural core from GTCRN_IVA and exports it to ONNX with recurrent caches, WITHOUT STFT/WPE/IVA/complex ops. The script will run on the user's Windows machine.
steps: Create tools/export_gtcrn_dual_core.py that: (a) imports GTCRN_IVA from gtcrn_iva.py, (b) loads checkpoint from checkpoints/best_model_0121.tar, (c) creates a GTCRNCoreWrapper nn.Module that receives spectral input [B, F, T, 2] (real+imag for 2 channels after WPE) and outputs enhanced spectrum [B, F, T, 2], extracting only the encoder/decoder/sequence_model layers from GTCRN_IVA, (d) exports via torch.onnx.export with dynamic axes for T, opset 17, with recurrent state caches as additional inputs/outputs following the same convention as the existing gtcrn.onnx (inputs[0]=mix, inputs[1..]=caches, outputs[0]=enh, outputs[1..]=updated_caches)

The wrapper class GTCRNCoreWrapper should: accept 2-channel spectral input (both channels post-WPE beamforming, so the network sees a beamformed single-channel spectrum), have the same STFT frame-by-frame interface as the mono model [1, 257, 1, 2] (real+imag per freq bin), so it integrates into the existing processOneFrame() infrastructure with minimal changes

Include validation in the script: run a dummy forward with T=1 (single frame) to verify the exported model produces correct output shape [1, 257, 1, 2], print input/output names and shapes, verify no complex ops or linalg.inv in the ONNX graph
Add usage instructions as docstring: how to run from H-GTCRN directory, what to copy to assets, expected output file name (gtcrn_dual_core.onnx)

The script must handle the case where GTCRN_IVA.forward() does STFT->WPE->IVA->encoder/decoder->iSTFT internally: the wrapper must bypass STFT/WPE/IVA and call only the encoder+decoder portion. If the internal structure uses self.encoder, self.decoder, self.sequence_model etc., extract those. If the architecture is a single forward with complex ops interleaved, provide guidance comments on which lines to modify in gtcrn_iva.py to make the core extractable.
acceptance_criteria: tools/export_gtcrn_dual_core.py exists and parses without syntax errors (ast.parse succeeds)
The script follows the same structure as the existing tools/export_gtcrn_script.py (load checkpoint, create model, export, validate)
The wrapper exposes the same [1, 257, 1, 2] input/output interface as the existing mono GTCRN model for compatibility with processOneFrame()
The script includes proper error handling for missing files, import errors, and shape mismatches
No torch.stft, torch.istft, torch.linalg.inv, or complex64 operations are used in the wrapper forward()
verification: Inspect the script for proper structure: wrapper class inheriting nn.Module, forward() with spectral I/O, torch.onnx.export call with opset_version>=17
findings: Script created at tools/export_gtcrn_dual_core.py (800 lines). AST parse passes. Wrapper class is AdaptiveGTCRNCore with forward(self, mix, conv_cache, tra_cache, inter_cache) signature. Uses auto-discovery of encoder/decoder/sequence_model layers with heuristic fallbacks. opset_version=17, dynamic T axis. Includes ONNX graph validation for forbidden ops. No torch.stft/istft/linalg.inv/complex64 in code. Manual mode available via --manual flag.
